core_mechanism: asymptotics of a family of orthogonal polynomials generated by a three-term (Lanczos) recurrence with respect to a discrete measure, where the polynomials' mass at large index redistributes according to the density of that measure and its support boundary, forcing a spreading quantity to saturate rather than grow indefinitely
brief_reason: The abstract states that orthogonal polynomials built on a discrete energy spectrum crowd away from low-density regions at large index, which provably halts the growth of the associated spread measure past a characteristic length — i.e. saturation driven by the large-order behavior of orthogonal polynomials on a discrete measure.
